title: Queering the Wilderness in a Debut Novel
description:
Melissa Faliveno’s debut, Hemlock, is a queer, atmospheric novel about addiction, memory, and the ways family history embeds itself in the body. The book follows Sam, a woman in her late thirties who leaves her life in Brooklyn to spend time alone at her family’s remote cabin in the Wisconsin Northwoods. The trip is meant […]

The post Queering the Wilderness in a Debut Novel appeared first on <
hook: A woman returns to her family cabin to confront addiction and inherited trauma—atmospheric, queer, and somatically precise about how the past lives in your body.

title: Tyrant Style
description: “This was proper writing, pretentious even. Style pervaded right down to the sentence, as if every piece was assembled from the best sentences its writer had or would ever come up with.”
hook: On writers whose sentences are so controlled they've stopped meaning anything—a trap disguised as ambition that kills the thing it claims to worship.

title: Podcast | Christopher Bollas
description:
‘I was not there to alleviate their suffering. I was there to listen to them.’

Christopher Bollas on psychoanalysis, its relationship to literature, and whether it can speak to the great crises of our time.

The post Podcast | Christopher Bollas appeared first on Granta.
hook: A psychoanalyst on listening without fixing, and whether therapy can address what literature already knows about human suffering.

title: If anyone can rebuild Evangelion, it's Yoko Taro
description: Beloved game director Yoko Taro is tackling a Neon Genesis Evangelion sequel. A look at his career shows why this is a dream pairing.
hook: Yoko Taro rebuilding Evangelion is a dream pairing—a look at why this particular director's obsessions match the anime's deepest ones.

title: If you don’t play, you can’t win: Desert Hearts at 40
description: Donna Deitch's 1986 lesbian romance is a fearless example of why it's so important the queer community are able to tell their own stories.
hook: Donna Deitch's 1986 lesbian romance proves why queer filmmakers telling their own stories isn't nice representation—it's a political necessity.

title: 7 Poetry Books That Expand What an Elegy Can Look Like
description:
An elegy, D. A. Powell writes in an essay titled “Structures of the Elegy,” is a “paradise of remembering.” Twelve years after my mother passed, as I began work on my book, Fifty Mothers, I kept asking myself, how could I converse with her through poems? How could I remember her, not just to mourn, […]

The post 7 Poetry Books That Expand What an Elegy Can Look Like appeared first
hook: Seven poetry collections that transform elegy from mourning ritual into a way of actually speaking to the dead—paradise through remembering.

title: Resident Evil Requiem is a timely critique of capitalist greed
description: Capcom takes a shot at selfishness and greed in Resident Evil Requiem's plot and Victor Gideon in particular
hook: Resident Evil Requiem's villain embodies capitalist greed in ways that feel urgent now—a horror game that knows what scares us most.
